item.title: 5 tourists killed in cave diving accident; search underway for bodies
item.description: The University of Genoa said the victims included a marine biology professor, her daughter and two young researchers.

item.title: Trump touts "fantastic trade deals" with Xi as Beijing summit ends
item.description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping concluded their summit in Beijing on Friday with both countries looking to claim the visit as a win.

item.title: Cartel leader "The Gardener" hit with U.S. charges after dramatic capture
item.description: Jalisco cartel boss Audias Flores Silva was arrested in an operation by Mexican Navy special forces based on information provided by U.S. agencies.

item.title: Oklahoma becomes latest state to sue Roblox over child safety concerns
item.description: Oklahoma has filed a lawsuit against Roblox, making it the latest state to take legal action against the popular gaming platform over child safety concerns.

item.title: 2 senators call on FAA to study impact of reduced flight attendant staffing
item.description: Sens. Tammy Duckworth​ of Illinois and Tammy Baldwin of Wisconsin say their concern is there may be more emergency exit doors than flight attendants in the event of an evacuation.

item.title: Ebola outbreak in Congo kills dozens, Africa's CDC says
item.description: A new Ebola outbreak in a remote Congo province has recorded 65 deaths so far and has 246 suspected cases, Africa CDC says.

item.title: RFK Jr. says he won't run for president in 2028
item.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is trying to address the interests of his MAHA supporters, who view him as their hope for the future, while being a good soldier in the eyes of the Trump White House.

item.title: FDA blocked melanoma drug as confusion reigned under Makary
item.description: A third of patients in a clinical trial had tumors shrink while taking a genetically engineered treatment known as RP1.

item.title: Meet the horses running in the Preakness Stakes
item.description: The Preakness Stakes will feature its biggest field in 15 years with 14 horses in the middle jewel of horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: How will Jerome Powell be remembered as he exits as Fed chair?
item.description: As Powell steps down after more than eight years leading the Federal Reserve, economists say he helped steer the U.S. through historic shocks but misread inflation.

item.title: Seized ship taken toward Iran as Trump and Xi agree on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A ship was taken by unknown parties toward Iranian waters after an Indian-flagged vessel was attacked off Oman.

item.title: ICE releases wife of U.S. Army soldier after monthlong detention
item.description: ICE has released the wife of an active-duty U.S. soldier after a month in detention, her husband told CBS News.

item.title: Why are so many U.S. CEOs in China with Trump, and what do they want?
item.description: More than a dozen American CEOs are accompanying President Trump on his trip to China. That's not unusual.

item.title: How a 3-legged dog and 3-legged cat found their forever home
item.description: Two animals with specials needs are adjusting to their new home in Maryland after recovering together at "Last Chance Animal Rescue." Photojournalist Parrish Smith shows us how Blueberry and Meadow found each other and their forever home.

item.title: Restaurants turning back the clock to lure customers
item.description: Some restaurants are going retro to try to bring customers into dining rooms. Bradley Blackburn got a taste of how they're putting nostalgia - on the menu.

item.title: Coast Guard seizes sailboat in Lynette Hooker's disappearance probe, sources say
item.description: The sailboat used by Brian and Lynette Hooker​ in their travels around the Bahamas — named "Soulmate" — has been seized by U.S. Coast Guard investigators.

item.title: North Texas man executed for 2004 killing of TCU professor
item.description: The execution came after a divided Supreme Court lifted a stay over his intellectual‑disability claims, clearing the way for Texas' 600th lethal injection since 1982.

item.title: What U.S. and China want out of talks between Trump and Xi
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and President Trump are discussing a wide range of topics in Beijing, including Taiwan and the war with Iran. Henrietta Levin, a senior fellow with the Freeman Chair in China Studies at CSIS, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss what success would look like for both the U.S. and China during these talks.

item.title: New Yellowstone spinoff "Dutton Ranch" centers on fan-favorite couple
item.description: Yellowstone actors Cole Hauser and Kelly Reilly are reprising their roles as the fan-favorite onscreen couple Rip Wheeler and Beth Dutton in the newest "Yellowstone" spinoff, "Dutton Ranch." CBS News' Julianne Ferreira has more.

item.title: Hero pilot recounts crash landing in Atlantic Ocean
item.description: Ian Nixon, a veteran pilot from the Bahamas, put the plane he was flying down in the ocean without anyone suffering serious injuries.

item.title: GOP Rep. Buddy Carter says Republicans need "best player on the field" in Georgia Senate primary
item.description: With less than a week to go before the GOP Georgia Senate primary, an Atlanta-Journal Constitution poll has the majority of voters undecided, making a runoff race in June increasingly likely. One of the candidates, Rep. Buddy Carter, joins "The Takeout" to discuss the state of the race.

item.title: American passenger from hantavirus-hit cruise ship on life in quarantine
item.description: The number of people being monitored for hantavirus in the United States has grown to 41, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said Thursday.

item.title: Rep. Lou Correa says "we owe it to our veterans" to research psychedelic drug therapies
item.description: In April, President Trump signed an executive order that speeds up research and increases funding for psychedelic drug therapies. Democratic Rep. Lou Correa of California, who is leading a bipartisan effort to advance these therapies, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Sen. Elissa Slotkin reacts to latest Iran news, Xi's warning to Trump on Taiwan
item.description: Taiwan, the Iran war and trade loomed over President Trump's meeting with China's Xi Jinping on Thursday. Democratic Sen. Elissa Slotkin of Michigan joins "The Takeout" to weigh in. Then, Lanhee Chen, fellow at the Hoover Institution, joins with analysis.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds mail access to abortion pill mifepristone for now
item.description: The Supreme Court has maintained mail access to the abortion pill mifepristone, setting aside for now a lower court order that blocked abortion providers from prescribing the widely used drug through telehealth and shipping it to patients.

item.title: FBI offers $200K for info on ex-Air Force officer accused of spying for Iran
item.description: The FBI said Thursday that it's still trying to locate Monica Witt, who is accused of defecting to Iran in 2013 and revealing highly classified U.S. intelligence.

item.title: 5/14: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Xi lays out warning for Trump on Taiwan; Chinese Americans discuss hopes for summit.

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will sue The New York Times
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government plans to file a defamation lawsuit against The New York Times.

item.title: Trump Mobile says its T1 mobile phone to ship this week
item.description: Trump Mobile's $499 gold-toned phone has faced delays since it was unveiled in June 2025.

item.title: Senate adopts resolution to withhold senators' pay during shutdowns
item.description: The Senate unanimously agreed to adopt a resolution on Thursday that will withhold senators' pay during a lapse in funding for any federal agency.
